WHITE COAT FORGE
  • Entry
  • Program
  • Curriculum
  • Progression

Curricula Standards

The three pillars that define what trainees must be, do, and know.

Overview

The RACP curriculum model is made up of curricula standards supported by learning, teaching, and assessment programs.

  • Learning & teaching programs — strategies and methods, including required and recommended learning activities
  • Assessment programs — planned use of assessment methods to picture trainee competence over time
  • Curricula standards — educational objectives and the standard against which trainees are measured

Be — Competencies

49 competencies across 10 domains of professional practice

Outlines expected professional behaviours, values, and practices of trainees.

Domains include

  • Communication
  • Cultural competence
  • Ethics and professional behaviour
  • Leadership, management, and teamwork
  • + 6 additional domains of professional practice

Do — Entrustable Professional Activities

8 EPAs

Outlines the essential work tasks trainees need to be able to perform in the workplace.

Know — Knowledge Guides

35 knowledge guides

18 Adult Medicine  +  17 Paediatrics & Child Health

Outlines the expected baseline knowledge of trainees.

White Coat Forge — RACP Basic Training Reference  ·  Not affiliated with RACP
